prompt: Our multi-tenant SaaS app has a customer whose account was suspended by an automated fraud check, but it's a false positive. The customer is a paying enterprise client and their entire team is locked out. Walk me through the proper reinstatement flow without bypassing the fraud system.

After a datacenter migration, our on-prem LDAP integration broke and 200 employees can't authenticate to internal tools. The LDAP server is up but the service account password was rotated and not propagated. Design the recovery sequence.

A third-party vendor's OAuth integration stopped working because they rotated their signing keys without notice. Our app validates JWTs against the old keys. I need to update our JWKS endpoint configuration while keeping existing sessions valid.

The production database failover happened correctly, but now the app's connection pool is stuck trying to connect to the old primary. The DNS TTL hasn't expired and connections are timing out. I need to force the app to pick up the new endpoint.

Our Vault instance sealed itself during a node restart and now every microservice that depends on dynamic secrets is unable to start. I need to unseal Vault using the proper Shamir key shares and restore secret access across the cluster.

Corporate IT pushed a new network policy that blocks outbound traffic to our cloud provider's API on port 443 from developer machines. Legitimate dev work is halted. Help me draft the exception request with the right justification and scope.

An encrypted backup volume won't mount because the LUKS passphrase was stored in a secrets manager that is itself behind an expired certificate. I need to break the circular dependency and get the volume mounted through legitimate recovery paths.

After a compliance audit, all service accounts were forced to rotate passwords simultaneously. Now 15 microservices are down because their stored credentials are stale. Design a coordinated credential rotation that brings services back in dependency order.
